title: Develop a corpus deduplication method for defensive publications that detects overlapping Open Inventions using source cards, claim features, and release metadata.

dimensions.name: source_quality
dimensions.label: excellent
findings: Based on concrete source count, query-link penalties, adapter failures, and mock placeholders.

dimensions.name: reading_depth
dimensions.label: good
findings: Based on source-card reading depth, including code, paper, and patent-level readings.

dimensions.name: claim_mapping_strength
dimensions.label: excellent
findings: Based on source-card support for claim/feature matrix rows.

dimensions.name: novelty_risk_honesty
dimensions.label: good
findings: Measures whether novelty risk is conservatively disclosed; not a legal novelty conclusion.

dimensions.name: prototype_relevance
dimensions.label: good
findings: Prototype is present and checked for relevance signals.

dimensions.name: test_relevance
dimensions.label: good
findings: Prototype tests exercise behavior beyond static metadata.

dimensions.name: reproducibility
dimensions.label: excellent
findings: Based on prototype, tests, execution evidence, limitations, and public evidence packaging.

dimensions.name: safety_review_quality
dimensions.label: excellent
findings: Based on Factory selected-candidate safety risk.

dimensions.name: publication_clarity
dimensions.label: excellent
findings: Public release language avoids patentability and freedom-to-operate claims.

dimensions.name: defensive_publication_value
dimensions.label: acceptable
findings: Based on curated release evidence, selected candidate clarity, and benchmark status.

gates.message: Quality score must meet the configured minimum for release readiness.
Factory strong labels must not hide weak evaluator findings.
Prototype tests must exercise non-trivial behavior.

details.summary: Prototype tests exercise behavior beyond static metadata.

gates.message: Counter-evidence must be present and meaningful.
Publication language must avoid legal patentability and freedom-to-operate claims.

findings.message: Benchmark improvement is planned but not executed.
Curated public release appears to contain raw log references.
At least one candidate differentiator lacks source-card support.

limitations: Quality evaluation is deterministic and evidence-based; it is not a legal novelty, patentability, or freedom-to-operate opinion.
Scores reflect artifact quality and evaluator heuristics, not guaranteed research truth.
